title: Japan and South Korea agree to strengthen defense cooperation.
summary: Defense ministers reaffirm denuclearization and play table tennis to improve relations.

title: Death of singer and actress Miwa Akihiro at age 91.
summary: Known for her song 'Yoitomake no Uta' and peace activism, she died of old age.

title: United States and Iran intensify mutual attacks in the Gulf.
summary: Both accuse each other of violating the ceasefire while bombings continue.

title: Fire at Osaka factory spreads to residential buildings.
summary: The fire burned approximately 1,000 square meters and left one person slightly injured.

title: South Korea eliminated from World Cup and president criticizes coach.
summary: President calls coach 'incompetent' after early group stage elimination.

title: Komae Mayor Toshio Matsubara wins reelection for third term.
summary: Defeats former representative of the 'Path of Regeneration' party in the election.

title: Nishikawa's grand slam drives Orix comeback over Rakuten.
summary: The four-base hit breaks the team's losing streak.

title: Japanese national team prepares for match against Brazil in World Cup.
summary: Players arrive in Brazil and analyze tactics, while Brazilian fans show confidence in their team.

title: Earthquake in Venezuela causes more than 1,400 deaths and major losses.
summary: Rescue teams search for thousands of missing while the economy takes a hard hit.

title: Current Kakogawa Mayor Yasuhiro Okada wins fourth reelection.
summary: Defeats a 29-year-old candidate in the municipal election.

title: Hanshin Tigers pitcher Haruto Takahashi achieves 10 consecutive wins.
summary: He is the first pitcher this season to reach 10 wins without a loss.

title: 'Hunter x Hunter' surpasses 100 million copies in circulation.
summary: Yoshihiro Togashi's manga reaches milestone with release of volume 39.

title: Japan plans first clinical trial of pig-to-human kidney transplant in 2028.
summary: Meiji University startup aims to solve donor shortage.

title: Messi sets record by scoring in seven consecutive World Cup matches.
summary: Argentine forward scores in his seventh consecutive match, surpassing historical marks.

title: Japanese politician denies that Princess Aiko can inherit the throne.
summary: Nakasone claims the princess will not find a husband, sparking debate on imperial succession.

title: Democratic Republic of Congo wins its first World Cup match.
summary: With a late comeback, it secures qualification to the round of 16 and eliminates South Korea.

title: Weak magnitude 5 earthquake shakes northeastern Japan.
summary: The tremor was felt in Iwate and Aomori prefectures without causing serious damage.

title: Chinese and Russian military aircraft conduct joint flight near Japan.
summary: The action is seen as a show of force that worries Tokyo.

title: Japanese rider Ai Ogura wins first MotoGP Grand Prix.
summary: It's the first win by a Japanese rider in the category in 22 years.

title: Heatwave in Europe leaves more than 1,300 dead.
summary: World Health Organization urges countries to take measures against extreme heat.
